title: EventSender type alias comment does not explain channel choice
description: In client_task.rs line 30: 'type EventSender = tokio::sync::mpsc::UnboundedSender<AppEvent>;' with the comment '// Alias to keep function signatures readable.' The comment explains why the alias exists but not why UnboundedSender was chosen over a bounded channel. A future contributor adding a new function that takes EventSender needs to know the channel is unbounded (send() never blocks, never fails due to capacity) to understand the backpressure characteristics. The send_event() helper's doc comment does explain the unbounded choice, but the type alias itself gives no hint. Adding a brief note at the alias (e.g. 'unbounded: send() is always non-blocking; see send_event() for rationale') would make the alias self-contained.
close_reason: Fixed: EventSender type alias now has a comment explaining the unbounded channel choice and rationale
